summary: The optimal way to assess body temperature quickly when dealing with severe hypothermia due to cold exposure at high altitudes involves using rectal thermometry because it balances between rapidity needed during emergencies while maintaining reasonable reliability despite potential delays over oral/axillary techniques; however, given practical considerations favoring quick implementation here too, rectally measured temp stands out even if not perfectly instantaneous versus specialized routes but superior overall utility under these conditions where time-to-treatment
